briefdescription.para: the type of elements stored in the cube
if eT is non-complex, pod_type is same as eT. otherwise, pod_type is the underlying type used by std::complex

memberdef.definition: arma_aligned const Mat<eT>** const Cube< eT >::mat_ptrs
memberdef.name: mat_ptrs

briefdescription.para: pointer to an array containing pointers to Mat instances (one for each slice)

memberdef.definition: arma_aligned const eT* const Cube< eT >::mem
memberdef.name: mem

briefdescription.para: pointer to the memory used by the cube (memory is read-only)
number of columns in each slice (read-only)
number of elements in the cube (read-only)
DEPRECATED: do not use this member variable it will be removed in version 3.0.
number of rows in each slice (read-only)
number of slices in the cube (read-only)
linear element accessor (treats the cube as a vector); no bounds check.
linear element accessor (treats the cube as a vector); no bounds check

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_row, const uword in_col, const uword in_slice)
memberdef.name: at

briefdescription.para: element accessor; no bounds check

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_row, const uword in_col, const uword in_slice) const
memberdef.name: at

briefdescription.para: element accessor; no bounds check
change the cube (without preserving data) to have the same dimensions as the given cube

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_rows, const uword in_cols, const uword in_slices)
memberdef.name: Cube

briefdescription.para: construct the cube to have user specified dimensions

memberdef.argsstring: (eT *aux_mem, const uword aux_n_rows, const uword aux_n_cols, const uword aux_n_slices, const bool copy_aux_mem=true, const bool strict=true)
memberdef.name: Cube

detaileddescription.para: construct a cube from a given auxiliary array of eTs. if copy_aux_mem is true, new memory is allocated and the array is copied. if copy_aux_mem is false, the auxiliary array is used directly (without allocating memory and copying). note that in the latter case the default is to copy the array.

memberdef.argsstring: (const eT *aux_mem, const uword aux_n_rows, const uword aux_n_cols, const uword aux_n_slices)
memberdef.name: Cube

detaileddescription.para: construct a cube from a given auxiliary read-only array of eTs. the array is copied.

briefdescription.para: construct a cube from a given cube

memberdef.argsstring: (const BaseCube< pod_type, T1 > &A, const BaseCube< pod_type, T2 > &B)
memberdef.name: Cube

briefdescription.para: construct a cube from a subview_cube instance (e.g. construct a cube from a delayed subcube operation)
create a cube from OpCube, i.e. run the previously delayed unary operations
create a cube from OpCube, i.e. run the previously delayed unary operations
create a cube from eOpCube, i.e. run the previously delayed unary operations
EXPERIMENTAL.
create a cube from Glue, i.e. run the previously delayed binary operations
create a cube from eGlue, i.e. run the previously delayed binary operations

param.type: const mtGlueCube< eT, T1, T2, glue_type > &

briefdescription.para: EXPERIMENTAL.

memberdef.argsstring: (const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T1 > &A, const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T2 > &B)
memberdef.name: Cube

param.type: const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T1 > &
const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T2 > &

briefdescription.para: for constructing a complex cube out of two non-complex cubes
fill the cube with the specified value

detaileddescription.para: print contents of the cube (to the cout stream), optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the precision and cell width are modified. on return, the stream's state are restored to their original values.

print contents of the cube to a user specified stream, optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the precision and cell width are modified. on return, the stream's state are restored to their original values.

print contents of the cube (to the cout stream), optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the stream's state are used as is and are not modified (i.e. the precision and cell width are not modified).

print contents of the cube to a user specified stream, optionally preceding with a user specified line of text. the stream's state are used as is and are not modified. (i.e. the precision and cell width are not modified).

briefdescription.para: returns true if the given index is currently in range
returns true if the given start and end indices are currently in range

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_row, const uword in_col, const uword in_slice) const
memberdef.name: in_range

briefdescription.para: returns true if the given location is currently in range

memberdef.argsstring: (const span &row_span, const span &col_span, const span &slice_span) const
memberdef.name: in_range

memberdef.argsstring: (const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T1 > &A, const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T2 > &B)
memberdef.name: init

param.type: const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T1 > &
const BaseCube< typename Cube< eT >::pod_type, T2 > &

briefdescription.para: for constructing a complex cube out of two non-complex cubes

inbodydescription.para: < compile-time abort if eT isn't std::complex
< compile-time abort if T is std::complex
< compile-time abort if types are not compatible

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword slice_num, const uword N, const bool set_to_zero=true)
memberdef.name: insert_slices

detaileddescription.para: insert N slices at the specified slice position, optionally setting the elements of the inserted slices to zero

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword row_num, const BaseCube< eT, T1 > &X)
memberdef.name: insert_slices

detaileddescription.para: insert the given object at the specified slice position; the given object must have the same number of rows and columns as the cube

briefdescription.para: returns true if the cube has no elements
returns true if all of the elements are finite

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::string name, const file_type type=auto_detect, const bool print_status=true)
memberdef.name: load

briefdescription.para: load a cube from a file

memberdef.argsstring: (std::istream &is, const file_type type=auto_detect, const bool print_status=true)
memberdef.name: load

briefdescription.para: load a cube from a stream
returns a pointer to array of eTs used by the cube

memberdef.name: memptr

briefdescription.para: returns a pointer to array of eTs used by the cube

memberdef.argsstring: (const uword in_rows, const uword in_cols, const uword in_slices)
memberdef.name: ones

briefdescription.para: in-place element-wise cube multiplication
in-place element-wise cube mutiplication (using a subcube on the right-hand-side)

param.type: const mtGlueCube< eT, T1, T2, glue_type > &

in-place cube element-wise multiplication, with the right-hand-side operand having delayed operations

in-place cube element-wise multiplication, with the right-hand-side operands having delayed operations

briefdescription.para: In-place multiplication of all elements of the cube with a scalar.

memberdef.name: operator++

briefdescription.para: prefix ++
postfix ++ (must not return the object by reference)

memberdef.definition: arma_inline const Cube< eT > & Cube< eT >::operator+=
memberdef.name: operator+=

briefdescription.para: In-place addition of a scalar to all elements of the cube.
in-place cube addition
in-place cube addition (using a subcube on the right-hand-side)

param.type: const mtGlueCube< eT, T1, T2, glue_type > &

detaileddescription.para: internal cube construction; if the requested size is small enough, memory from the stack is used. otherwise memory is allocated via 'new'
try to steal the memory from a given cube; if memory can't be stolen, copy the given cube

briefdescription.para: Dense cube class.
